# TRIGGER-GATE-DOCTRINE

## Current public-safe doctrine
- trigger should be understood primarily as routing/gating logic
- historical stored trigger assets may still exist in private baseline libraries, but runtime doctrine has shifted toward matched-trigger and gate behavior
- trigger is conceptually distinct from the host hook itself
- host hook = runtime entrypoint
- UMG trigger/gate = routing decision
- output transform = post-trigger behavior selection

## First-release public position
This repo exposes trigger/gate doctrine, not private runtime histories.
Examples and richer gate inventories remain intentionally limited until they are curated cleanly.
